summary:
The option stating that UPT is unnecessary cannot be correct as it neglects critical monitoring needed during such an advanced pregnancy; all else respects proper protocol but may require nuanced judgment regarding specific circumstances like requesting abortion or obtaining necessary evidence from a genital exam under appropriate conditions.
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two concise sentences focusing only on key clinical points related to the provided scenario:

The refusal to perform Universal Prenatal Testing (UPT) at 22 weeks' gestation overlooks essential surveillance crucial for managing high-risk pregnancies involving minors who are victims of violence. Other actions respect procedural guidelines yet still necessitate careful consideration of the adolescent's wishes concerning potential intervention versus support through available resources post-assessment. \boxed{} To ensure clarity while adhering strictly within your specified constraints: 

Not performing UPT at 22 weeks ignores critical oversight needs in late-term pregnancies where patients have been sexually assaulted. Proper handling includes allowing supervised examinations consistent with consent principles despite complex emotional contexts surrounding these cases.\boxed{}
This summary captures core elements about necessity vs optional procedures directly pertinent to the case details presented.
